News 📰️
1.)
Top 5 Most Clicked News Articles From Last Week
A.)
Jensen Huang: NVIDIA – The $4 Trillion Company & the AI Revolution | Lex Fridman Podcast #494
| Lex Fridman | YouTube.com | March 23, 2026
Jensen Huang, co-founder and CEO of NVIDIA, explores the transformative potential of AI, highlighting NVIDIA’s pivotal role in the ongoing AI revolution. The conversation delves into key challenges in AI scaling, supply chains, and the future trajectory of technology.
B.)
Terence Tao — How The World’s Top Mathematician Uses AI
| Dwarkesh Patel | YouTube.com | March 20, 2026
Kepler’s groundbreaking methods for discovering the laws of planetary motion exemplify the complexities surrounding scientific validation, revealing that the pursuit of knowledge can often take centuries. Terence Tao explores how the verification process for theories is fraught with challenges, illustrating the nuanced interplay between established knowledge and emerging scientific insights, especially in the realm of AI.
C.)
Anthropic Made a Safer OpenClaw & More AI News You Can Use
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | March 20, 2026
Igor introduces Claude Dispatch, a safer and more user-friendly version of OpenClaw, while discussing competitive offerings from Perplexity and Manus. The latest updates to Google Workspace and Google Maps are also highlighted, providing a comprehensive overview of significant advancements in the AI landscape.
D.)
Google AI Studio Got a Big Upgrade and We’re All Vibecoders Now
| AI For Humans | YouTube.com | March 20, 2026
Google’s latest upgrade to AI Studio introduces significant capabilities that enhance the vibecoding experience, including support for databases, multiplayer functionality, and persistent sessions. This evolution positions Google AI Studio in a competitive landscape as it integrates new features and tools that could redefine workflows and collaboration in AI development.
E.)
Do This With OpenClaw So You Don’t Fall Behind… (14 Use Cases)
| Matt Berman | YouTube.com | March 18, 2026
Explore a series of innovative use cases for OpenClaw that can enhance productivity for agents and streamline operations. Strategies covered include threaded chats, voice memos, and security best practices, providing essential tools to stay ahead in a competitive landscape.
March 30, 2026 📅️
2.)
The AI Image Platform That Does What Others Can’t (Try It FREE!)
| Theoretically Media | YouTube.com | March 30, 2026
Recraft V4 introduces significant advancements in AI image generation, featuring a comprehensive comparison with Nano Banana. The session showcases the innovative Recraft Studio, along with new vector and SVG capabilities, along with cutting-edge node-based workflows and OpenClaw integrations that enhance user experience.
3.)
Top 15 Breakthrough Discoveries That Scientists Say Will Change Medicine Forever
| AI Uncovered | YouTube.com | March 30, 2026
Groundbreaking medical advancements are set to revolutionize healthcare, with discoveries ranging from gene therapies to AI-driven diagnostics. Experts reveal how these innovations could transform prevention, diagnosis, and treatment, potentially reshaping everyday life for countless individuals.
4.)
Bluesky Users Revolt Against AI Tool Attie, Blocking It More Than ICE and White House Accounts
| Decrypt.co | March 30, 2026
A newly launched AI tool on Bluesky, named Attie, has been blocked over 125,000 times, reflecting user pushback against automation and concerns about the platform’s direction. Attie allows users to create custom feeds based on their interests, but many believe it undermines the original appeal of Bluesky as a refuge from AI-driven social media.
5.)
Anthropic’s Success Sparks Server Crunch
| TheInformation.com | March 30, 2026
Anthropic is experiencing significant success, leading to a surge in demand for servers to support its operations. This has sparked a broader concern in the tech industry regarding server availability and scalability as companies rush to keep up with AI advancements.
6.)
Instacart Introduces ‘Physical AI’ Smart Carts To Influence Grocery Shopping In Real Time
| Retail-Insider.com | March 30, 2026
Instacart has unveiled its innovative Caper Carts equipped with Physical AI technology, which processes data in real time to enhance grocery shopping experiences. This system allows retailers to optimize shopping based on customer behavior and location within the store, leading to increased basket sizes and improved customer interactions.
7.)
Audit Claude Platform Activity With The Compliance API
| Claude.com | March 30, 2026
The Compliance API allows administrators to programmatically access audit logs across organizations using the Claude Platform, enhancing security and compliance by tracking user activities and changes. It supports filtered access to logs based on activity types and user specifications, crucial for organizations in regulated industries.
8.)
Introducing Multi-Model Intelligence In Researcher
| Techcommunity.microsoft.com | March 30, 2026
Microsoft 365 Copilot’s Researcher has launched new multi-model capabilities named Critique and Council, enhancing research quality through distinct AI roles for generation and review. The architecture allows for better accuracy, depth, and insights by evaluating complex tasks more effectively than traditional single-model systems.
9.)
Copilot Cowork: Now Available In Frontier
| Microsoft.com | March 30, 2026
Microsoft announces the launch of Copilot Cowork as part of the Frontier program, designed to assist with multi-step work processes within Microsoft 365. This AI integration aims to enhance productivity by facilitating task delegation and management through intelligent workflows that leverage enterprise data securely.
10.)
The Feud Running The AI Industry
| TheNeuronDaily.com | March 30, 2026
A deep dive into the feud between OpenAI and Anthropic, exploring the personal and ideological split that occurred following key disagreements on AI development principles. The article highlights continued competitive tensions and fundamental differences in approach to safety and responsibility in AI deployment between the two companies.
March 29, 2026 📅️
11.)
27 Claude Code Tips You Didn’t Know About (In 12 Minutes)
| Jono Catliff | YouTube.com | March 29, 2026
Unlock the potential of Claude Code with 27 essential tips garnered from over 500 hours of practical experience. From cloning websites to automating tasks, this concise guide reveals techniques to streamline workflows and enhance productivity, ensuring you make the most of your time with Claude.
12.)
Almost Timely News: Terraforming the AI Use Case Desert
| Chris Penn | YouTube.com | March 29, 2026
Exploring the untapped potential of AI in the workplace, Christopher Penn highlights strategies to delegate less desirable tasks to generative AI tools. The discussion includes practical insights on using programs like Openwork and Minimax M2.7 for effective job automation and a case study illustrating successful implementation.
13.)
Claude Cowork Tutorial From Cowork’s Design Lead (40 Min) | Jenny Wen
| Peter Yang | YouTube.com | March 29, 2026
Jenny Wen, the design lead at Cowork, shares insights into Anthropic’s workflow and how Cowork facilitates product design. Delve into the transformative aspects of design at Anthropic, including a live demo of turning user feedback into actionable product insights and a glimpse into the prototypes that never made it to launch.
14.)
The Hidden Industry That Controls The Tech Your Company Uses
| The Neuron | YouTube.com | March 29, 2026
Victoria Durgin and Katie Bavoso from Channel Insider join Corey Noles and Grant Harvey to unveil the complexities of “the channel,” a vast distribution network that underpins AI technology acquisition for businesses. Their insights illuminate how the evolving landscape of AI impacts business strategies in 2026, emphasizing the necessity for companies to navigate this hidden industry effectively.
15.)
Anthropic Leaked Claude Mythos. Cybersecurity Stocks Crashed
| TheNeuronDaily.com | March 29, 2026
The latest revelations from Anthropic regarding its new AI model, Claude Mythos, have caused significant turbulence in the cybersecurity market, with stocks falling sharply following news of its capabilities and leak. The model is reported to surpass existing technology, raising concerns over cybersecurity implications and operational costs for users.
16.)
Helping Disaster Response Teams Turn AI Into Action Across Asia
| OpenAI.com | March 29, 2026
In a collaborative effort with the Gates Foundation, ADPC, and DataKind, OpenAI hosted an inaugural AI Jam for Disaster Management in Bangkok, gathering 50 leaders from across Southeast and South Asia. The workshop focused on leveraging AI to enhance disaster response effectiveness, addressing critical challenges faced in resource-constrained environments as Asia remains the world’s most disaster-prone region.
March 28, 2026 📅️
17.)
Claude Code: Build Your Full AI Marketing Team (Agents + Claude Skills)
| Grace Leung | YouTube.com | March 28, 2026
Learn how to build an efficient AI marketing team using Claude Code, featuring five specialized agents and twelve collaborating skills designed for comprehensive campaign management. The tutorial takes viewers through the step-by-step process of creating, configuring, and utilizing AI tools for effective marketing strategies.
18.)
How AI Is Now Making Scientific Discoveries Humans Can’t
| AI Uncovered | YouTube.com | March 28, 2026
Artificial intelligence is pioneering scientific breakthroughs previously thought unattainable, leveraging its data-processing capabilities to reveal hidden patterns and complexities across various research fields. Significant advancements in areas such as medicine and physics demonstrate how AI enhances hypothesis generation and problem-solving, transforming the landscape of scientific inquiry.
19.)
Anthropic’s Claude Popularity With Paying Consumers Is Skyrocketing
| TechCrunch.com | March 28, 2026
Anthropic’s AI product, Claude, is experiencing a surge in paying subscribers, with sales more than doubling this year. An analysis of consumer transactions indicates that both new and returning users are engaging with the service following successful marketing efforts, including humorous Super Bowl commercials that positioned Claude favorably against competitors.
March 27, 2026 📅️
20.)
AI News: Anthropic Went Crazy This Week!
| Matt Wolfe | YouTube.com | March 27, 2026
Breaking developments in AI technologies emerge as Anthropic announces significant updates and various challenges in the sector. With insights into Claude’s capabilities and Google’s latest expansions, the episode covers a range of pivotal topics shaping the future of artificial intelligence.
21.)
ARC AGI 3 Just Dropped, What It Means For AGI
| Matt Berman | YouTube.com | March 27, 2026
The release of ARC AGI 3 marks a significant leap in the development of artificial general intelligence, paving the way for new opportunities and applications in the field. Experts analyze its implications and potential return on investment for businesses looking to leverage AI technologies effectively.
22.)
Design Your Dream Room By Shopping With ChatGPT
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | March 27, 2026
ChatGPT transforms the shopping experience by allowing users to upload design inspiration and discover similar items effortlessly. Explore innovative ways to visualize your dream room, making decor selection more accessible and enjoyable than ever.
23.)
Find The Perfect Gift By Shopping With ChatGPT
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | March 27, 2026
ChatGPT offers an innovative approach to gift shopping, enabling users to discover ideal presents tailored to their budget and preferences. By utilizing visual browsing and conversational refinement, users can seamlessly find products that match their inspirations and requirements.
24.)
How Surge AI Finds AI’s Real Weakness
| The Neuron | YouTube.com | March 27, 2026
Corey Noles and Grant Harvey engage with Nick Heiner, VP of Product at Surge AI, to explore reinforcement learning environments—key areas where AI models are trained and tested. The conversation addresses the significant challenges faced by top-performing models, insights from Wall Street evaluations of leading AI systems, and a bold prediction about the future of workforce automation.
25.)
How to Be “Agent Native” in 2026 (w/ Every CEO Dan Shipper)
| The Neuron | YouTube.com | March 27, 2026
Corey Noles and Grant Harvey engage with Dan Shipper, the CEO of Every, to explore the concept of agent-native engineering and its implications for software development. Shipper shares insights from the viral success of the AI document editor Proof and discusses how Every’s agile team continues to deliver innovative AI tools that reshape the industry.
26.)
New ChatGPT Library Explained & More AI News You Can Use
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | March 27, 2026
Igor highlights the newly introduced ChatGPT Library feature, which allows users to effectively organize and manage their files within the interface. The discussion also includes updates on Google AI Studio’s overhaul, the lackluster release of Midjourney V8, and various advancements in the AI landscape.
27.)
OpenAI’s Path to AGI: Cancel Sora, Launch a Potato
| AI For Humans | YouTube.com | March 27, 2026
OpenAI has undergone a significant strategic shift, entirely abandoning the Sora project and its partnership with Disney, as well as phasing out other initiatives like Citrus Mode. The company is now focusing on enterprise solutions and a new AI model named Spud, which is projected to enhance economic growth.
28.)
Tesla and SpaceX Alumni on Elon Musk, Decision Velocity, and the Future of Hard Tech | a16z
| a16z | YouTube.com | March 27, 2026
Chandler Luzsicza and Turner Caldwell share invaluable lessons learned from their experiences at Tesla and SpaceX, focusing on decision velocity, flat organizational structures, and effective milestone setting. They provide a glimpse into the demanding culture of innovation and the strategies essential for cultivating high-performing teams in the hard tech sector.
29.)
Google Built Real-Life Pied Piper
| TheNeuronDaily.com | March 27, 2026
Google introduced Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a groundbreaking voice model, and a compression algorithm named TurboQuant, which enhances AI performance significantly. Additionally, Apple plans to allow rival AI assistants access to Siri, ending exclusivity with OpenAI, which highlights the intensifying competition in AI technology.
30.)
Meshy And MakerWorld Team Up To Put AI 3D Model Generation In Bambu Lab Users’ Hands
| 3DPrintingIndustry.com | March 27, 2026
Bambu Lab’s MakerWorld has integrated Meshy, a generative AI platform for 3D model creation, allowing users to convert photos into print-ready 3D models quickly. The updated Multi-Color Printing feature simplifies the process by automating color assignments, enhancing accessibility for casual 3D printing enthusiasts.
31.)
MLB Pitches AI-Powered Commentary In Its Play-By-Play App
| Blog.google | March 27, 2026
MLB has introduced its new Scout Insights feature, providing fans with AI-generated commentary in the Gameday feed of its app. Utilizing Gemini and Google Cloud AI, this feature offers in-depth insights and engaging facts during games, enhancing the overall fan experience.
March 26, 2026 📅️
32.)
Claude Code Just Got Another Huge Upgrade (Computer Control)
| Jono Catliff | YouTube.com | March 26, 2026
Jono explores the capabilities of Claude Code’s updated Computer Mode, which enables control over keyboard, mouse, and screenshots via a smartphone. Notable applications include remote video exports from Premiere Pro, automatic system settings adjustments, and app management directly from a mobile device.
33.)
New Claude Cowork Projects Explained In 9 Minutes
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | March 26, 2026
Igor offers a comprehensive guide on utilizing the innovative Projects feature in Claude Cowork, showcasing its powerful capabilities and functionality. The insights provided equip viewers with the knowledge they need to enhance their collaborative efforts and optimize their workflow.
34.)
The AI Film Workflow No One Is Talking About…Yet
| Theoretically Media | YouTube.com | March 26, 2026
Gain insights into the innovative AI filmmaking process through a detailed breakdown of the production of “Dragon Blue.” Explore every aspect from pre-production to final delivery, highlighting essential tools, techniques, and valuable lessons learned along the way.
35.)
This Datacenter Problem Nobody’s Talking About
| Matt Wolfe | YouTube.com | March 26, 2026
The ongoing Datacenter Moratorium presents significant concerns that are often overlooked. A critical analysis reveals the implications of this issue on the tech landscape and the broader impact it may have on future infrastructures.
36.)
Two AI Models Set to “Stir Government Urgency”, But Will This Challenge Undo Them?
| AI Explained | YouTube.com | March 26, 2026
OpenAI’s new Spud model and Anthropic’s anticipated releases aim to provoke increased governmental action amidst the unveiling of the ARC-AGI-3 benchmarks. The implications of these advanced AI models raise essential questions about their role and the potential challenges they may face in shaping the future of artificial intelligence by 2026.
37.)
What Codex Unlocks for Ryan Hendler, Dev at Me&U
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | March 26, 2026
In this engaging session, Ryan Hendler from me&u shares the transformative impact of Codex on his workflow. With newfound efficiency, he dedicates his afternoons to team collaboration and customer engagement, achieving what was previously unfeasible.
38.)
You Need To Try These Open-Source AI Projects Right Now…
| Matt Berman | YouTube.com | March 26, 2026
Explore innovative open-source AI projects that can transform and elevate your business operations. From enhancing productivity to streamlining workflows, these tools are essential for those looking to stay competitive in an AI-driven landscape.
39.)
Play The Puzzle That Broke Every AI Model
| TheNeuronDaily.com | March 26, 2026
The ARC Prize Foundation launched ARC-AGI-3, a new benchmark testing AI’s ability to learn in unfamiliar environments, where leading AI models scored below 1%. In contrast, human testers achieved a 100% success rate, prompting discussions about the methodologies used to evaluate AI intelligence.
40.)
Anthropic Left Details Of An Unreleased Model, Invite-Only CEO Retreat, Sitting In An Unsecured Data Trove In A Significant Security Lapse
| Fortune.com | March 26, 2026
AI company Anthropic inadvertently revealed details of an upcoming model release and an exclusive CEO event due to a security lapse that left internal documents publicly accessible. The issue was attributed to human error in the configuration of the company’s content management system.
41.)
David Sacks Is No Longer The White House AI And Crypto Czar
| TheVerge.com | March 26, 2026
David Sacks, previously serving as the White House AI and Crypto Czar, announced his transition to a special advisory council role to advise the Trump administration on technology issues. His tenure was marked by significant involvement in shaping AI policies, but his approach led to some political backlash and challenges within the White House.
42.)
Make The Switch: Bring Your AI Memories And Chat History To Gemini
| Blog.google | March 26, 2026
Gemini has introduced features that allow users to import their AI memories and chat histories from other applications, facilitating a seamless transition without starting from scratch. Users can now capture important preferences and continue conversations directly within the Gemini app.
43.)
OpenAI Shelves Erotic Chatbot ‘Indefinitely’
| TheVerge.com | March 26, 2026
OpenAI has decided to pause its plans for an adult mode in ChatGPT, shifting focus back to core products amid concerns from employees and investors about the implications of sexualized AI content on society. This halt follows OpenAI’s discontinuation of its text-to-video platform, Sora, due to internal discussions regarding priorities and potential societal impacts.
44.)
Cohere Launches An Open Source Voice Model Specifically For Transcription
| TechCrunch.com | March 26, 2026
Cohere has introduced Transcribe, an open source automatic speech recognition model that enhances tasks like note-taking and speech analysis. With 2 billion parameters, it claims to outperform several existing models, boasting a low word error rate while supporting 14 languages. The model is available for free and intended for those with consumer-grade GPUs.
45.)
Wikipedia Bans AI-Generated Articles
| TheVerge.com | March 26, 2026
Wikipedia has updated its guidelines to prohibit editors from writing or rewriting articles with AI, limiting AI’s usage to basic copy editing and translations. This decision arises from concerns that AI-generated content could breach core Wikipedia policies, prompting the need for stricter regulations.
46.)
Mistral TTS: The Future of Text-to-Speech Models
| Mistral.ai | March 26, 2026
Mistral AI has launched Voxtral TTS, a leading text-to-speech model featuring state-of-the-art multilingual voice generation with 4 billion parameters. This new model promises realistic speech in 9 languages, low latency, and extensive customization for enterprises, enhancing voice agent workflows and user interactions.
47.)
Introducing Tribe V2: A Predictive Foundation Model Trained To Understand How The Human Brain Processes Complex Stimuli
| AI.meta.com | March 26, 2026
Meta has unveiled TRIBE v2, a cutting-edge model that serves as a digital twin of human neural activity, boasting a significant increase in prediction resolution. This advancement enables neuroscientists to test hypotheses on brain responses to stimuli without human subjects, thereby accelerating neuroscience research and improving the development of AI systems.
48.)
Search Live Is Expanding Globally
| Blog.Google | March 26, 2026
Google’s Search Live feature has expanded to all languages and locations where AI Mode is available, enabling users in over 200 countries to interact with Search through voice and camera. Powered by the new Gemini 3.1 Flash Live model, users can hold conversations in their preferred languages, enhancing their search experience and accessibility.
49.)
Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making Audio AI More Natural and Reliable
| Blog.google | March 26, 2026
Gemini 3.1 Flash Live is Google’s latest audio model designed to enhance the naturalness and reliability of AI-driven voice interactions. With improved precision, tonal understanding, and enhanced processing speed, it enables developers and enterprises to create more fluid and effective voice agents while being accessible for users across over 200 countries.
50.)
Announcing Babylon.js 9.0
| Blogs.windows.com | March 26, 2026
Babylon.js 9.0 introduces significant improvements, including a new Clustered Lighting system that enhances performance with many lights in a scene. The update also features a Node Particle Editor for creating complex particle systems and Volumetric Lighting for realistic effects. Enhanced animation retargeting capabilities allow animations to be shared across different character models, making it a powerful tool for web developers.
51.)
Anthropic Wins Injunction In Court Battle With Trump Administration
| WSJ.com | March 26, 2026
The legal victory for Anthropic allows the AI startup to continue its operations without interference from the Trump administration, reinforcing its position within the competitive landscape of artificial intelligence advancement. This case highlights the ongoing clashes between government regulations and technological innovation.
52.)
Claude AI Maker Anthropic Said To Weigh Ipo As Soon As October
| Bloomberg.com | March 26, 2026
Anthropic, the company behind Claude AI, is reportedly considering an initial public offering (IPO) that could take place as early as October. This move could potentially reshape the competitive landscape of the AI industry as companies seek to capitalize on growing investment interest.
March 25, 2026 📅️
53.)
8 Things Your Claude Code App Needs Before Making Money
| Jono Catliff | YouTube.com | March 25, 2026
Unlock the potential of your SaaS applications with eight essential components necessary for success, covering everything from user authentication to payment processing. Gain insights on optimizing the development and deployment processes to ensure your app meets industry standards and provides a seamless user experience.
54.)
AI Tool Better Than OpenClaw? + NVIDIA’S $1T Prediction & AI Image Wars
| The Next Wave | YouTube.com | March 25, 2026
Hosts Matt Wolfe and Joe Fier analyze NVIDIA’s pivotal role in the AI industry following their recent GTC conference, exploring groundbreaking AI tools for businesses and impactful robotics advancements. The discussion includes Jensen Huang’s significant $1 trillion chip sales forecast and the implications of AI data accessibility for users and enterprises.
55.)
Anthropic Won’t Stop Shipping. Good Luck, Everyone Else.
| AI For Humans | YouTube.com | March 25, 2026
Anthropic’s latest updates reveal significant advancements in AI agents, including Claude’s ability to operate computers from phones and the introduction of Claude Dispatch. The discussion explores the implications for the evolving open versus closed AI landscape, highlighting key developments and notable uses by industry leaders.
56.)
Episode 15 – Inside The Model Spec
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | March 25, 2026
OpenAI researcher Jason Wolfe joins host Andrew Mayne to provide insights into the Model Spec, a framework guiding AI behavior. The discussion covers practical applications, the hierarchy in model instructions, and how the framework adapts to feedback and technological advancements.
57.)
The Wildest AI Film You’ll See Today! (Seedance 2.0)
| Theoretically Media | YouTube.com | March 25, 2026
“Dragon Blue” delivers an electrifying blend of action and style, centering on themes of vengeance and katanas. Each stunning visual was crafted using Seedance 2.0, showcasing a unique artistic vision that transcends traditional filmmaking.
58.)
Sora Lasted 6 Months. Disney’s $1B Deal Lasted 3.
| TheNeuronDaily.com | March 25, 2026
OpenAI has shut down its video app Sora just six months after its launch, along with its $1 billion partnership with Disney. Meanwhile, OpenAI is focusing on a new AI model, codenamed ‘Spud,’ while pressing forward with efforts to raise capital and enhance its data center infrastructure.
59.)
Senate Democrats Are Trying To ‘Codify’ Anthropic’s Red Lines On Autonomous Weapons And Mass Surveillance
| TheVerge.com | March 25, 2026
Senator Adam Schiff is drafting a bill to establish regulations for AI usage in autonomous weapons and mass surveillance, reflecting concerns over Pentagon operations. The legislation aims to ensure human oversight in life-and-death decisions and is part of broader discussions surrounding Anthropic’s restrictions on its technology’s military applications.
60.)
Sanders And AOC Unveil Data Center Moratorium Bill
| Axios.com | March 25, 2026
Sen. Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez are set to announce legislation that would impose a nationwide pause on new data center construction until federal safeguards for AI are established. This aggressive stance is part of their broader effort to push for strong regulations around AI’s rapid growth and its impacts on workers, consumers, and the environment.
61.)
Lyria 3 Pro: Create Longer Tracks In More Google Products
| Blog.google | March 25, 2026
Google has launched Lyria 3 Pro, a music generation model that enables users to create longer tracks up to three minutes with customizable elements like verses and choruses. This advanced model integrates into various Google products, allowing enhanced music production and experimentation in tools like Vertex AI, Google AI Studio, and Google Vids.
62.)
Trump To Name Mark Zuckerberg, Larry Ellison And Jensen Huang To Tech Panel
| WSJ.com | March 25, 2026
Former President Donald Trump is set to appoint several high-profile tech leaders, including Mark Zuckerberg and Larry Ellison, to a new advisory panel focused on technological advancement and policy. The panel aims to bridge the gap between technology and governance amid growing concerns about regulation and innovation.
March 24, 2026 📅️
63.)
7 Techniques That Make Claude Code Websites Look $10,000
| Jono Catliff | YouTube.com | March 24, 2026
Uncover the secrets to elevating AI-generated websites with skilled enhancements that create a polished, professional appearance. By employing innovative techniques for design, animation, and typography, ensure your Claude Code website stands out in a crowded digital landscape.
64.)
An Updated Shopping Experience In ChatGPT
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | March 24, 2026
ChatGPT has transformed online shopping, introducing enhanced visual product browsing, side-by-side comparisons, and up-to-date information for better purchasing decisions. The expansion of the Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP) ensures that merchants can showcase their products with fresh data, optimizing visibility, ranking, and conversion rates.
65.)
Hard Takeoff Has Started
| Matt Berman | YouTube.com | March 24, 2026
The latest developments from Higgsfield highlight the initiation of Hard Takeoff, showcasing its significant implications for future AI advancements. Experts share insights into the expansive capabilities of AI models that are now accessible, paving the way for innovative applications across various fields.
66.)
How To Run Open Source AI Models
| Tina Huang | YouTube.com | March 24, 2026
Tina provides an in-depth guide on utilizing open-source AI models, covering all the essential methods for implementation and usage. Gain valuable insights that can elevate your understanding and application of AI technologies in various contexts.
67.)
OpenAI Just Killed Sora
| Matt Wolfe | YouTube.com | March 24, 2026
Sora’s official shutdown marks a major development in the AI landscape, prompting questions about the implications for users and the industry at large. This announcement highlights critical updates and what it means for the evolving world of artificial intelligence.
68.)
Seedance 2.0 Has Released (Kinda…)
| Theoretically Media | YouTube.com | March 24, 2026
Seedance 2.0 has emerged amid uncertainty and limited availability in just seven countries. The new model introduces significant guardrails, including restrictions on real faces and C2PA watermarking, shaping its Western release potential. In tandem, Luma Labs unveils Uni One, enhancing the creative possibilities for video production from initial concept to storyboard.
69.)
The 10 Technologies That Will Outlive AI (Most People Don’t Know About These)
| AI Uncovered | YouTube.com | March 24, 2026
Innovations beyond artificial intelligence are emerging that have the potential to outlast AI itself. From groundbreaking energy systems to advanced biotechnology, these technologies promise to redefine various aspects of human life, impacting everything from medicine to transportation for generations to come.
70.)
What Codex Unlocks for Notion
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | March 24, 2026
Ryan Nystrom shares insights on how Codex has transformed his workflow, enabling him to create Notion AI Voice Input independently while leading his team. This accomplishment exemplifies the power of Codex in facilitating innovative solutions in a collaborative environment.
71.)
Bernie Sanders Interviewed Claude On Camera: Here’s What Happened
| TheNeuronDaily.com | March 24, 2026
Senator Bernie Sanders conducted an interview with Claude, an AI chatbot, focusing on issues of AI and privacy. The interview attracted significant attention, revealing how AI responses can vary based on the user’s identity and concerns raised about data privacy and ethical AI regulations.
72.)
TurboQuant: Redefining AI Efficiency With Extreme Compression
| Research.Google | March 24, 2026
This article introduces TurboQuant, a new quantization algorithm that significantly reduces the size of large language models without impacting accuracy. Along with its supporting algorithms, Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss and PolarQuant, TurboQuant optimizes memory usage in vector searches and enhances performance in key-value caches, achieving superior results in various benchmarks.
73.)
Disney Exits OpenAI Deal After AI Giant Shutters Sora
